firecrawlQueryPatterns: new open source AI coding agent tool github 2026

voice.engineLicense: GPL-3.0 (engine, shelled-out-to as external process, not statically linked); original rhasspy/piper (MIT) is archived read-only as of Oct 2025
voice.voiceLicense: MIT (rhasspy/piper-voices MODEL_CARD, per research doc -- not re-verified by direct MODEL_CARD fetch, flagged as Open Gap in research/channels/code-trail-topics-voice-music.md)
voice.note: en_US-lessac-high is the documented fallback in the same MIT-licensed family if Ryan's tone doesn't land in production auditioning.

music.note: Copyright Risk Flag in research doc leaves trending-vs-royalty-free as an explicit operator decision (same framing as mind-mosaic). Pipeline currently only wires a synthesized placeholder pad, not a sourced track -- see synthesize_music_bed() in pipeline/run_channel.py.

sfx.source: @rse/soundfx (CC-0 / CC-BY-3.0), individual soundfx.d/*.mp3 files vendored locally

visualTokens.notes: All hex values sampled directly from research/visual-reference/code-trail.md's Extracted Color Tokens table (PIL-sampled frames of the downloaded thebrainmaze sorting-algorithm reference clip), not invented. captionEmphasis (#20009E, saturated violet-blue) is the highest-hue-band sample in that table; moodboard explicitly notes 'no secondary/tertiary brand color -- all color budget goes into the data itself,' so emphasis/keyword-tint colors deliberately reuse the same hue-sweep palette as the mood accents rather than introducing a new brand color.

typography.captionStyle: clean humanist/grotesk UI sans, medium weight, mixed/Title Case (not all-caps) -- exact family unverified per moodboard Open Gap
typography.captionRevealUnit: word-by-word for KineticCaption beats; line-by-line for CodeBlock beats
typography.emphasisTier: two-tier: default white body text, one saturated violet-blue (#20009E) emphasized keyword/token per caption or code beat

spacing.infographicReveal: staggered/incremental, one element per speech beat, not all-at-once
spacing.brollDensity: moderate-to-high per moodboard: one persistent dashboard-chrome element (here: CodeBlock's header row) plus one large focal visualization, two-layer density ceiling

pacing.note: Platform composition FPS is fixed at 60 (src/remotion/Root.tsx), independent of the moodboard reference clip's own 30fps source -- same fixed-60fps convention used by mind-mosaic. Reference clip measured 0 cuts across 121s (continuous single-shot); adapted target for Shorts-native pacing here is a new visual beat roughly every 2-4s, per moodboard Motion-Personality Direction, not the reference's own ~25-30s macro-beat cadence.

motionPersonality.easingNote: simple eased transitions (easeOut/easeInOut) throughout, no bounce/overshoot spring moves -- reserved only for a single hook-moment punch at video start, per moodboard Motion-Personality Direction. Continuous micro-motion (slow hue-cycle) used for chrome accents only, not body/code text, to preserve legibility.
